title: Rich Roasted French Lentil Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful stew from Normandy featuring Puy lentils, cooked to perfection in a cataplana vessel, and finished with a touch of brininess and flaky texture, perfect for a pescatarian diet
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large cataplana vessel, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the chopped carrots and celery and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the Puy lentils, sea salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the white wine and fish stock, and bring to a boil.
Cover the cataplana vessel with parchment paper, creating a papillote, and transfer to the preheated oven.
Roast for 30 minutes, then remove the parchment paper and continue roasting for an additional 10 minutes.
Remove from the oven and let cool slightly before serving.
